steps.title: Compare the comfort ratings
steps.content: The table shows that all three participants wanted the room cooler, but their comfort ratings were not the same. Participant 20 reported minus 2, while participants 1 and 21 each reported plus 1.

steps.title: Use the colder outlier
steps.content: The matching choice captures that contrast: participant 20 felt much colder than the other two even while sharing their cooler preference. Choice A says the ratings were the same, Choice B uses information not shown, and Choice D reverses the comparison. The answer is C.
